title: Plan a Stage 5 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 5 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 5 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 5 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Digit” mean?
options: A single symbol from 0 to 9
What a digit is worth from its position
The dot separating wholes from parts of a whole
The first / second places after the decimal point
questions.answer: A single symbol from 0 to 9
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digit means A single symbol from 0 to 9.

questions.question: What does “Place value” mean?
options: What a digit is worth from its position
The dot separating wholes from parts of a whole
The first / second places after the decimal point
Make a number simpler by moving it to a near, tidy value
questions.answer: What a digit is worth from its position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Place value means What a digit is worth from its position.

questions.question: What does “Polygon” mean?
options: A closed 2D shape with straight sides
A corner / corners of a shape
All sides and angles equal / not all equal
3 equal / 2 equal / 0 equal sides
questions.answer: A closed 2D shape with straight sides
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Polygon means A closed 2D shape with straight sides.

questions.question: What does “Vertex / Vertices” mean?
options: A corner / corners of a shape
All sides and angles equal / not all equal
3 equal / 2 equal / 0 equal sides
Under 90° / exactly 90° / over 90° angle
questions.answer: A corner / corners of a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Vertex / Vertices means A corner / corners of a shape.

questions.question: What does “Sequence” mean?
options: A list of numbers following a rule
One number in a sequence
How to get from one term to the next
How to find any term from its position (nth term)
questions.answer: A list of numbers following a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sequence means A list of numbers following a rule.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: One number in a sequence
How to get from one term to the next
How to find any term from its position (nth term)
The fixed step in a linear sequence
questions.answer: One number in a sequence
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means One number in a sequence.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information collected as numbers
A single value representing a whole set of data
The value that appears most often
The middle value when data is in order
questions.answer: Information collected as numbers
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information collected as numbers.

questions.question: What does “Average” mean?
options: A single value representing a whole set of data
The value that appears most often
The middle value when data is in order
Total of the values divided by how many there are
questions.answer: A single value representing a whole set of data
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Average means A single value representing a whole set of data.

questions.question: What does “Decimal number” mean?
options: A number with a point showing parts of a whole
The dot between the wholes and the parts
The first and second places after the point
A sensible guess before calculating exactly
questions.answer: A number with a point showing parts of a whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Decimal number means A number with a point showing parts of a whole.

questions.question: What does “Decimal point” mean?
options: The dot between the wholes and the parts
The first and second places after the point
A sensible guess before calculating exactly
A number greater than zero
questions.answer: The dot between the wholes and the parts
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Decimal point means The dot between the wholes and the parts.

questions.question: What does “Face” mean?
options: A flat surface of a 3D shape
A line where two faces meet
A corner point where edges meet
6 square faces / 6 rectangular faces
questions.answer: A flat surface of a 3D shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Face means A flat surface of a 3D shape.

questions.question: What does “Edge” mean?
options: A line where two faces meet
A corner point where edges meet
6 square faces / 6 rectangular faces
Same shape all along its length
questions.answer: A line where two faces meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Edge means A line where two faces meet.

questions.question: What does “Fraction” mean?
options: A number showing part of a whole
The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
A number using a point to show tenths, hundredths, thousandths
questions.answer: A number showing part of a whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Fraction means A number showing part of a whole.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
A number using a point to show tenths, hundredths, thousandths
A number out of 100, shown with %
questions.answer: The top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Probability” mean?
options: How likely an event is, from 0 to 1
One possible result of an experiment
All outcomes have the same chance
An outcome you are counting as a success
questions.answer: How likely an event is, from 0 to 1
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Probability means How likely an event is, from 0 to 1.

questions.question: What does “Outcome” mean?
options: One possible result of an experiment
All outcomes have the same chance
An outcome you are counting as a success
A test to see what actually happens
questions.answer: One possible result of an experiment
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Outcome means One possible result of an experiment.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Different fractions naming the same amount (1/2 = 2/4)
A denominator two fractions share
questions.answer: The top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Denominator” mean?
options: The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Different fractions naming the same amount (1/2 = 2/4)
A denominator two fractions share
Divide top and bottom by the same number to reach lowest terms
questions.answer: The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Denominator means The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole.

questions.question: What does “Angle” mean?
options: The amount of turn between two lines at a point
The unit for measuring an angle
The point where the two arms meet
One of the two lines forming the angle
questions.answer: The amount of turn between two lines at a point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Angle means The amount of turn between two lines at a point.

questions.question: What does “Degree (°)” mean?
options: The unit for measuring an angle
The point where the two arms meet
One of the two lines forming the angle
A tool marked in degrees for measuring angles
questions.answer: The unit for measuring an angle
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Degree (°) means The unit for measuring an angle.

questions.question: What does “Product” mean?
options: The answer to a multiplication
The answer to a division
The number being divided
The number you divide by
questions.answer: The answer to a multiplication
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Product means The answer to a multiplication.

questions.question: What does “Quotient” mean?
options: The answer to a division
The number being divided
The number you divide by
A number that divides exactly into another
questions.answer: The answer to a division
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Quotient means The answer to a division.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information collected about the world
How many times a value or category occurs
The most common value
Highest value minus lowest value
questions.answer: Information collected about the world
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information collected about the world.

questions.question: What does “Frequency” mean?
options: How many times a value or category occurs
The most common value
Highest value minus lowest value
The total shared equally (the average)
questions.answer: How many times a value or category occurs
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Frequency means How many times a value or category occurs.

questions.question: What does “Ratio” mean?
options: A part-to-part comparison, written with a colon
One of the numbers in a ratio
Divide all terms by the same number to lowest terms
Different ratios showing the same relationship
questions.answer: A part-to-part comparison, written with a colon
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Ratio means A part-to-part comparison, written with a colon.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: One of the numbers in a ratio
Divide all terms by the same number to lowest terms
Different ratios showing the same relationship
A part-to-whole comparison (fraction, decimal or %)
questions.answer: One of the numbers in a ratio
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means One of the numbers in a ratio.

questions.question: What does “Perimeter” mean?
options: The distance around the outside edge of a shape
The amount of flat space inside a shape
A square one unit wide, used to measure area
The bottom side of a triangle / the straight distance up from it
questions.answer: The distance around the outside edge of a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Perimeter means The distance around the outside edge of a shape.

questions.question: What does “Area” mean?
options: The amount of flat space inside a shape
A square one unit wide, used to measure area
The bottom side of a triangle / the straight distance up from it
A shape made from two or more simple shapes
questions.answer: The amount of flat space inside a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Area means The amount of flat space inside a shape.

questions.question: What does “Unit fraction” mean?
options: A fraction with 1 on top (1/2, 1/3, 1/5)
Top number / bottom number of a fraction
A number with a decimal point (0.5, 1.4, 12.9)
The first place after the decimal point
questions.answer: A fraction with 1 on top (1/2, 1/3, 1/5)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Unit fraction means A fraction with 1 on top (1/2, 1/3, 1/5).
questions.unitTitle: Multiplying and Dividing Fractions and Decimals

questions.question: What does “Numerator / Denominator” mean?
options: Top number / bottom number of a fraction
A number with a decimal point (0.5, 1.4, 12.9)
The first place after the decimal point
Adding the same number several times
questions.answer: Top number / bottom number of a fraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator / Denominator means Top number / bottom number of a fraction.
questions.unitTitle: Multiplying and Dividing Fractions and Decimals

questions.question: What does “Duration / Interval” mean?
options: The amount of time between two times
The time that has passed
1 to 12 with am (morning) or pm (afternoon)
00:00 to 23:59, no am or pm needed
questions.answer: The amount of time between two times
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Duration / Interval means The amount of time between two times.

questions.question: What does “Elapsed time” mean?
options: The time that has passed
1 to 12 with am (morning) or pm (afternoon)
00:00 to 23:59, no am or pm needed
A chart of when events happen
questions.answer: The time that has passed
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Elapsed time means The time that has passed.

questions.question: What does “Commutative law” mean?
options: Order does not matter for + and ×
Grouping does not matter for + and ×
Spread a multiplication over a sum or difference
Brackets, Indices, Div/Mult, Add/Sub
questions.answer: Order does not matter for + and ×
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Commutative law means Order does not matter for + and ×.
questions.unitTitle: Number and the Laws of Arithmetic

questions.question: What does “Associative law” mean?
options: Grouping does not matter for + and ×
Spread a multiplication over a sum or difference
Brackets, Indices, Div/Mult, Add/Sub
( ) meaning 'do this part first'
questions.answer: Grouping does not matter for + and ×
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Associative law means Grouping does not matter for + and ×.
questions.unitTitle: Number and the Laws of Arithmetic

questions.question: What does “Coordinates” mean?
options: A pair (x, y) that fixes a point
Horizontal / vertical number lines
Where the axes cross: (0, 0)
One of the four regions of the grid
questions.answer: A pair (x, y) that fixes a point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coordinates means A pair (x, y) that fixes a point.

questions.question: What does “x-axis / y-axis” mean?
options: Horizontal / vertical number lines
Where the axes cross: (0, 0)
One of the four regions of the grid
A value left of or below the origin
questions.answer: Horizontal / vertical number lines
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: x-axis / y-axis means Horizontal / vertical number lines.
